title: oxybasis urbica subsp. rhombifolia
intro: Oxybasis urbica subsp. rhombifolia, also known as red-veined orache, is a subspecies of the Oxybasis urbica plant, belonging to the amaranth family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Oxybasis urbica subsp. rhombifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized for a range of health benefits.
